title: Cylinder test in rats
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7jti3","text":"Protocol for performing the cylinder test in rats. The cylinder test evaluates asymmetry in forelimb use, and can be used in animal models with unilateral lesions of the nigrostriatal dopaminergic pathway.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring cages to the behavioral room for at least 01:00:00 prior to the test for habituation

Place each rat in a glass cylinder (20-cm wide), surrounded by mirrors to allow a full 360 degree view. Videotape from the front for 00:05:00 or until the rat performs a minimum of 20 weight-bearing forepaw contacts with the cylinder walls.
Perform blinded analysis from the recorded videos of the number of contralateral forepaw contacts, and express these as a percentage of total forepaw contacts.
creator: María Sanchiz Calvo
